items.title: Warren Buffett on the market today: 'It's tough to find values when everybody is preferring gambling'
items.summary: The investor has been sharply critical of the stock market, one that he says is increasingly defined by speculative trading as opposed to long-term investing.

items.title: Wholesale prices unexpectedly declined 0.3% in June on big drop in gasoline
items.summary: As with consumer prices, the index benefited from easing energy costs, particularly as oil fell due to the brief pause in tensions between the U.S. and Iran.

items.title: New York Fed President Williams says inflation has peaked, rates 'well positioned'
items.summary: Williams cited five reasons why he expects the latest price surge has run its course.

items.title: DTCC, Wall Street’s post-trade powerhouse, tests tokenized markets with industry heavy hitters
items.summary: The DTCC is pushing to bring tokenization of real world assets from concept to reality using existing financial infrastructure.

items.title: ‘Arsenal of democracy’: Jamie Dimon announces $24 million effort to boost American shipbuilding
items.summary: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon unveiled a $24 million package to bolster American shipbuilding, funding a new submarine facility at the Philadelphia Navy Yard.

items.title: Watch Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh testify live before Senate banking committee
items.summary: Part of congressionally mandated Capitol Hill appearances for the central bank leader, Warsh spoke Tuesday to the House Financial Services Committee.

items.title: U.S. launches fresh wave of strikes on Iran, as analysts warn conflict risks becoming 'forever war'
items.summary: Fighting in the Middle East has intensified in recent days, but could escalate further if Iran fails to cooperate, President Donald Trump said.

items.title: Morgan Stanley posts record quarterly revenue and profit as equities trading surges 69%
items.summary: Like at peers Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase, a massive beat in equities trading drove the quarter's outsized results.

items.title: Mortgage rates rise to highest level in nearly a year, causing homebuyers to pause
items.summary: Mortgage rates moved higher last week, causing buyers to pull back, but refinancing did see small gains.

items.title: IRS chief Frank Bisignano will lead Trump Accounts expansion
items.summary: The Treasury Department is putting a top official in charge of the new program as it enrolls millions of families.

items.title: Hassett sees no 'excuse' to raise rates, says Warsh will push Fed to 'right answer'
items.summary: Hassett, on CNBC, credited President Donald Trump's policies after the latest consumer price index reading came in much lower than expected.

items.title: Stripe, Advent make $53 billion takeover offer for PayPal, sending stock soaring
items.summary: Stripe and Advent International have made a joint offer to acquire PayPal for $60.50 per share, in a deal that would valued at more than $53 billion.

items.title: Star analyst Dan Ives forms Yorkville Ives merchant bank after leaving Wedbush
items.summary: The new firm, Yorkville Ives & Co., will combine investment banking, equity research, institutional trading and principal investing.

items.title: Goldman Sachs’ former top lawyer tells House Epstein was a ‘masterful liar’ who used her to bolster his standing
items.summary: Kathryn Ruemmler said earlier this year she would leave Goldman Sachs at the end of June after fallout over her emails with Jeffrey Epstein.

items.title: Todd Blanche defends handling of Jeffrey Epstein files in Senate confirmation hearing
items.summary: Todd Blanche previously served as a criminal defense lawyer for President Donald Trump.

items.title: Warren Buffett calls Bill Gates' actions with Epstein 'distasteful' but says people make mistakes
items.summary: The Berkshire Hathaway chairman left out the Gates Foundation from his annual charitable stock gifts for the first time in 20 years.

items.title: Jim Cramer's top 10 things to watch in the stock market Wednesday
items.summary: ASML delivered a blowout quarter, and the June wholesale inflation report was softer than expected.

items.title: Americans say they need $1.2 million to retire comfortably, survey finds — but many expect to fall short
items.summary: Just 30% of workplace retirement plan investors think they'll reach $1 million before retiring, a new survey finds. Here's what's getting in their way.

items.title: Alibaba’s U.S.-listed shares rise 4% after Qwen AI set to be integrated in Apple Intelligence
items.summary: The Cyberspace Administration of China on Wednesday included Apple's AI services on a list of approved providers.

items.title: ASML rises 3% after hiking sales forecast for second time this year on strong AI chip demand
items.summary: ASML on Wednesday raised its guidance for a second time this year as its customers continue to ramp up their production capacity of AI chips.

items.title: Bipartisan group of senators propose Social Security reform process ahead of funding shortfall
items.summary: Social Security's trust fund that helps pay retirement benefits may run out in about six years. A bipartisan group of senators is calling for earlier reform.

items.title: Warsh pledges Fed policy 'regime change' to rid inflation 'tax' on American people
items.summary: Warsh pledged Tuesday to "get monetary policy right" and defeat the inflation that has bedeviled the central bank for the past five years.

items.title: The AI boom just found two new winners: Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase
items.summary: Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan showed that Wall Street is a major beneficiary of the AI boom, with record revenue driven by surging trading and investment banking.

items.title: Current and former employees sue Meta, alleging discrimination in using AI to conduct layoffs
items.summary: The lawsuit filed by current and former Meta employees underscores rising concerns about AI's impact on jobs and people with disabilities in the workforce.

items.title: Lucid dismisses report that it is weighing filing for bankruptcy or going private after shares plunge
items.summary: The report said Lucid was considering options that could include going private or filing for bankruptcy protection.

items.title: New York becomes first U.S. state to impose AI data center ban
items.summary: New York became the first state to ban construction of 'hyperscale' AI data centers, for one year, after Gov. Kathy Hochul signed an executive order Tuesday.

items.title: Apple in talks with startup that shrinks AI models to run on an iPhone
items.summary: PrismML says its compressed version of Alibaba’s Qwen model uses up to 15 times less memory, potentially advancing Apple’s AI push.

items.title: Renewed Hormuz hostilities drive ECB rates rethink amid ‘extremely volatile’ outlook
items.summary: Several consecutive days of strikes exchanged between the U.S. and Iran have cast uncertainty on the European Central Bank's interest rate decision next week.

items.title: Jim Cramer says concerns about AI market froth are overblown. Here's why
items.summary: CNBC's Jim Cramer said today's stock market is far less concerning than it was during the dot-com bubble.

items.title: Why Europe is suddenly betting big on drones
items.summary: Drones and autonomous systems are moving from niche battlefield tools to a core part of modern warfare.
